title: Anthropic Tool Use & Computer Use
learningObjectives: Master Anthropic's tool use protocol differences from OpenAI
Implement multi-turn tool conversations with Claude
Understand Claude's Computer Use API for GUI automation
Build a production tool-use pipeline with Claude
Handle Claude-specific behaviors: extended thinking, cache control

sections.title: Why Anthropic Tool Use Matters
sections.content: Anthropic's Claude has distinct strengths: stronger instruction following, better reasoning on complex tasks, and more cautious behavior on sensitive tasks. Claude's tool use protocol differs from OpenAI in important ways that trip up developers. Additionally, Anthropic introduced Computer Use — the ability for Claude to see a screenshot and control a computer (click, type, scroll) just like a human would. This enables a new class of automation: GUI automation that works on any application without custom APIs. Understanding both tools deepens your ability to choose the right model and capabilities for each task.

sections.title: Anthropic Tool Use Protocol
sections.content:
**Key differences from OpenAI (reviewed from Day 16):**

| Aspect | OpenAI | Anthropic |
|--------|--------|----------|
| Schema key | `parameters` | `input_schema` |
| Tool wrapper | `{type:'function', function:{...}}` | Direct object |
| Tool call block | `message.tool_calls[].function.arguments` (JSON string) | `content[].input` (already dict) |
| Tool result | `{role:'tool', tool_call_id, content}` | `{role:'user', content:[{type:'tool_result', tool_use_id, content}]}` |
| Stop reason | `finish_reason == 'tool_calls'` | `stop_reason == 'tool_use'` |
| Force specific tool | `tool_choice: {type:'function', function:{name}}` | `tool_choice: {type:'tool', name:'X'}` |

**Tool use response content structure:**
```python
# Claude's response when calling a tool:
response.content = [
    TextBlock(type='text', text='I will look up the weather...'),  # optional thinking
    ToolUseBlock(
        type='tool_use',
        id='toolu_01abc123',
        name='get_weather',
        input={'city': 'Paris', 'unit': 'celsius'},  # dict, not JSON string!
    )
]
response.stop_reason  # 'tool_use'
```

**Cache control (Anthropic-specific, reduces cost on repeated contexts):**
```python
# Mark a large system prompt or context for caching
{'type': 'text', 'text': '...long system prompt...', 'cache_control': {'type': 'ephemeral'}}
# Cached tokens: billed at 10% of normal input cost
# Cache lifetime: 5 minutes
```

**Extended Thinking (Claude 3.7+):**
```python
client.messages.create(
    model='claude-opus-4-7',
    max_tokens=16000,
    thinking={'type': 'enabled', 'budget_tokens': 10000},  # internal reasoning budget
    messages=[...]
)
# response.content[0] is a ThinkingBlock with the full reasoning trace
# response.content[1] is the actual TextBlock answer
```

**Computer Use:**
```python
# Available on Claude 3.5 Sonnet (claude-sonnet-4-6)
tools = [
    {'type': 'computer_20241022', 'name': 'computer',
     'display_width_px': 1024, 'display_height_px': 768,
     'display_number': 1},
    {'type': 'text_editor_20241022', 'name': 'str_replace_editor'},
    {'type': 'bash_20241022', 'name': 'bash'},
]
# Claude can: screenshot → click(x,y) → type(text) → scroll → key()
# You implement the actual computer control (pyautogui, Playwright)
```

sections.title: Anthropic Tool Use — Complete Implementation

items.mistake: Using 'parameters' instead of 'input_schema' in Anthropic tool definitions
items.why: OpenAI uses 'parameters'. Anthropic uses 'input_schema'. Using the wrong key silently ignores the schema definition, causing validation failures or wrong model behavior.
items.fix: Anthropic tool schema always uses `'input_schema'`, not `'parameters'`. Build or use an adapter function to convert between formats.

items.why: Unlike OpenAI where `tc.function.arguments` is a JSON string, Anthropic's `block.input` is already a Python dict. Calling json.loads() on a dict raises a TypeError.
items.fix: Use `block.input` directly: `fn(**block.input)`. No json.loads() needed for Anthropic.

items.mistake: Not passing all content blocks in the assistant turn
items.why: If the model returned both a TextBlock and ToolUseBlock, you must include both when appending the assistant turn. Dropping the TextBlock breaks the message history.
items.fix: Always append `response.content` (the full list) as the assistant message content, not just the tool_use blocks.

items.mistake: Missing the 'tool_result' wrapper when submitting tool outputs
items.why: OpenAI uses `{role: 'tool', tool_call_id, content}`. Anthropic requires tool results inside a user message: `{role: 'user', content: [{type: 'tool_result', tool_use_id, content}]}`.
items.fix: Anthropic tool results always go in a user message as a list with `type: 'tool_result'` items. Get tool_use_id from `block.id`.

sections.title: Interview Questions

conceptual.question: What is Anthropic's cache_control and how does it reduce costs?
conceptual.answer: Cache control marks specific content blocks (typically large system prompts or document contexts) for server-side caching. When the same cached prefix appears in subsequent requests within 5 minutes, the cached tokens are charged at 10% of normal input cost instead of 100%. To use: add `'cache_control': {'type': 'ephemeral'}` to the content block you want cached. Best for: large system prompts (>1000 tokens), document contexts passed repeatedly, few-shot examples. Only effective when the same prefix is reused across multiple requests — single requests don't benefit.
followUps: How does Anthropic's cache differ from OpenAI's automatic prompt caching?

conceptual.question: What is Claude Computer Use and what are its main use cases?
conceptual.answer: Computer Use is a capability where Claude can see a screenshot and emit computer control actions: click(x,y), type(text), scroll, key press. Your code captures the screenshot, sends it to Claude, receives an action, executes it (via pyautogui or a browser driver), takes another screenshot, and loops. Use cases: GUI automation for applications without APIs (legacy software), automated QA testing across visual interfaces, web scraping where the UI changes frequently. Current limitations: slow (each iteration requires an API call), expensive (image tokens), error-prone with complex UIs, not suitable for high-frequency automation.
followUps: When would you prefer Playwright over Claude Computer Use for web automation?

codeBased.question: Write the message structure for a two-turn Anthropic conversation where Claude calls a tool and you provide the result.
codeBased.answer:
```python
# Turn 1: User asks, Claude responds with tool_use
msgs = [{'role': 'user', 'content': 'What is the weather in Tokyo?'}]
response = client.messages.create(model=MODEL, max_tokens=512, tools=TOOLS, messages=msgs)

# Turn 2: Append assistant turn (ALL content blocks)
msgs.append({'role': 'assistant', 'content': response.content})

# Execute tool + append user turn with results
results = []
for block in response.content:
    if block.type == 'tool_use':
        result = get_weather(**block.input)  # block.input is dict
        results.append({'type': 'tool_result', 'tool_use_id': block.id, 'content': json.dumps(result)})

msgs.append({'role': 'user', 'content': results})

# Turn 3: Claude generates final answer
final = client.messages.create(model=MODEL, max_tokens=512, tools=TOOLS, messages=msgs)
print(final.content[0].text)
```

seniorScenario.question: You need to choose between OpenAI's function calling and Anthropic's tool use for a production customer support system. What factors guide the decision?
seniorScenario.answer: Key dimensions: (1) Quality of tool selection: test both on your specific task. Claude tends to be more conservative — it's less likely to call unnecessary tools and asks for clarification when the task is ambiguous. (2) Latency: GPT-4o-mini is generally faster than Claude Haiku for tool-heavy tasks. (3) Cost: compare (model price × avg tokens per session) including tool call overhead. (4) Specific capabilities: if you need Computer Use or extended thinking, Claude is the only option. If you need the Assistants API (managed threads, Code Interpreter), OpenAI is the choice. (5) Compliance: some enterprises have vendor exclusivity requirements. (6) Reliability: run both for a week in shadow mode (same queries, different model, human-evaluated quality), pick the better one. (7) Multi-model strategy: use both with a routing layer — GPT-4o-mini for standard queries, Claude Opus for complex reasoning. Build a provider-agnostic tool schema layer so you can switch providers without rewriting tools.
followUps: How would you implement A/B testing between OpenAI and Anthropic tool implementations?

wrongAnswers: Anthropic tool results use the same format as OpenAI — they use different message structures; Anthropic requires tool results inside a user message content array with type:'tool_result'
block.input in Anthropic tool use is a JSON string like OpenAI's arguments — block.input is already a Python dict; do not call json.loads() on it
cache_control permanently caches the content on Anthropic's servers — it's ephemeral with a 5-minute TTL; the cache expires if not accessed within that window

questions.question: In Anthropic's tool use, what is the correct key for defining a tool's parameter schema?
questions.explanation: Anthropic uses 'input_schema' while OpenAI uses 'parameters'. This is the most common source of bugs when migrating between providers.

questions.question: After Claude emits a tool_use block, where do the tool results go in the next API request?

options.A: As a 'tool' role message with tool_call_id
options.B: Inside a user message's content array as {type: 'tool_result', tool_use_id, content}
options.C: As an additional field in the last assistant message

questions.explanation: Anthropic tool results are embedded in a user message: {role: 'user', content: [{type: 'tool_result', tool_use_id: block.id, content: result_string}]}. The tool_use_id links the result back to the specific tool call.

questions.question: What does `cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'}` do in an Anthropic API call?

options.A: Prevents the response from being logged
options.B: Marks a content block for server-side caching — cached tokens cost 10% of normal input price for 5 minutes
options.C: Enables streaming for the marked content block
options.D: Deletes the content from Anthropic's servers after the request

questions.explanation: cache_control marks content for ephemeral caching. If the same prefix appears in subsequent requests within 5 minutes, it's a cache hit — those tokens cost 10% of normal input price instead of 100%. Best for large repeated contexts.

questions.question: What is Anthropic's Computer Use and how does the control loop work?

options.A: A tool for controlling IoT devices via Claude
options.B: Claude can see a screenshot and emit GUI actions (click, type, scroll) — you execute the actions and send a new screenshot back
options.C: A way to execute shell commands directly from Claude
options.D: A computer vision tool for analyzing screenshots without control capability

questions.explanation: Computer Use: (1) take screenshot → send to Claude. (2) Claude emits action (click at x,y, type text, etc.). (3) Your code executes the action (pyautogui, Playwright). (4) Take new screenshot, send back. Repeat until task complete. Claude sees and controls the GUI.

questions.question: Unlike OpenAI's `tc.function.arguments`, what type is Anthropic's `block.input`?

options.A: A JSON string that must be parsed with json.loads()
options.B: A Python dict ready to unpack with **
options.C: A list of argument name-value tuples

questions.explanation: Anthropic's block.input is already a Python dict — just call fn(**block.input). OpenAI's tc.function.arguments is a JSON string that requires json.loads(). This is a common bug when mixing the two APIs.
